title: Morgan Wallen Cancels Pittsburgh Concert Due to Severe Weather Threats
summary: The country singer was set to take the stage at Acrisure Stadium on June 6.
body: Morgan Wallen was forced to cancel a scheduled Pittsburgh tour stop due to severe weather threats. On Saturday afternoon (June 6), the 33-year-old country star took to social media to announce that he would no longer be performing that evening at Acrisure Stadium as part of his Still the Problem Tour. Related Morgan Wallen Jokes About Piano-Flipping Incident in Humorous Video: ‘Can’t You Tell I’m So Distraught’ Why Austin Neal’s Agency Has Attracted Morgan Wallen, Riley Green and Ella Langley: ‘How Can We Make Their Day As Easy As Possible?’ Morgan Wallen Pledges $1.2 Million to Upgrade His High School’s Baseball Field “After talking with local officials and my team, there is no choice but to cancel tonight’s show due to severe weather conditions expected throughout the rest of the day and night,” Wallen wrote in a statement shared on Instagram . “Safety for my fans and crew is the highest priority.” The “Last Night” singer concluded his message by informing fans that refunds would be available through their original point of purchase.
